epics.title: Multi-tenant foundation and company setup

stories.title: Tenant-aware authentication and membership

tasks.title: Tenant-aware authentication and membership
Users can authenticate and resolve one or more company memberships.
Every tenant-owned API request is scoped by company context.
Unauthorized access to another company’s data returns forbidden/not found.
Membership roles are persisted and available to authorization checks.
Use shared-schema multi-tenancy with `company_id` enforcement.
Keep auth provider abstraction for future SSO.
Prefer policy-based authorization in ASP.NET Core.

stories.title: Company workspace creation and onboarding wizard

tasks.title: Company workspace creation and onboarding wizard
User can create a company with name, industry, business type, timezone, currency, language, and compliance region.
Setup wizard persists progress and supports resume.
Industry/business templates can prefill recommended defaults.
Successful completion lands the user in the web dashboard with starter guidance.
Blazor web is the primary onboarding surface.
Store branding/settings in JSONB where flexibility is needed.
Keep template model extensible without code changes.

stories.title: Human user invitation and role assignment

tasks.title: Human user invitation and role assignment
Owner/admin can invite users by email to a company.
Invited users receive a pending membership until accepted.
Roles such as owner, admin, manager, employee, finance approver, and support supervisor are assignable.
Role changes take effect on subsequent authorization checks.
Invitation delivery can use outbox + background dispatcher.
Support re-invite and revoke flows.
Do not couple human roles to agent permissions.

stories.title: Baseline platform observability and operational safeguards

tasks.title: Baseline platform observability and operational safeguards
API exposes health endpoints for app, database, Redis, and object storage dependencies.
Structured logs include correlation IDs and tenant context where applicable.
Unhandled exceptions are captured and mapped to safe API responses.
Background job failures are logged and retryable.
Separate technical logs from business audit events.
Add rate limiting hooks early for chat/task endpoints.
Use outbox for reliable side effects.

epics.title: AI workforce and policy configuration

stories.title: Agent template catalog and hiring flow

tasks.title: Agent template catalog and hiring flow
System provides seed templates for at least finance, sales, marketing, support, operations, and executive assistant roles.
User can create an agent from a template and customize name, avatar, department, role, personality, and seniority.
Hired agents appear in the company roster with active status by default.
Template defaults are copied into company-owned agent configuration records.
Seed data should be versioned/migrated.
Avatar can initially be URL/file reference only.
Avoid hardcoding role behavior outside config where possible.

stories.title: Agent operating profile management

tasks.title: Agent operating profile management
Agent profile supports editing objectives, KPIs, role brief, tool permissions, data scopes, approval thresholds, escalation rules, trigger logic, and working hours.
Changes are persisted with updated timestamps and reflected in subsequent orchestration runs.
Invalid configurations are rejected with field-level validation.
Agent status can be changed to active, paused, restricted, or archived.
Store flexible policy/config fields in JSONB with server-side validation.
Restrict archived agents from new task assignment.
Keep config history auditable later via audit events.

stories.title: Autonomy levels and policy guardrails

tasks.title: Autonomy levels and policy guardrails
Agents support autonomy levels 0-3 with conservative defaults at 0 or 1.
Policy engine evaluates read/recommend/execute scope, thresholds, and approval requirements before tool execution.
Actions outside scope are blocked and logged with a reason.
Sensitive actions above threshold create approval requests instead of executing directly.
Guardrails must run pre-execution, not only post-response.
Keep policy decisions structured for auditability.
Default-deny when policy config is missing or ambiguous.

stories.title: Agent roster and profile views

tasks.title: Agent roster and profile views
Web roster lists agents with name, role, department, status, autonomy level, and workload/health summary.
Agent detail view shows identity, objectives, permissions, thresholds, and recent activity.
Users can filter roster by department and status.
Restricted fields/actions are hidden or disabled based on human role.
Blazor SSR first; add interactivity only where needed.
Health summary can initially be derived from task state and last activity.
Keep profile page as the anchor for future analytics.

epics.title: Knowledge, memory, and retrieval

stories.title: Company document ingestion and storage

tasks.title: Company document ingestion and storage
Users can upload documents with title, type, and access scope metadata.
Files are stored in object storage and document metadata in PostgreSQL.
Ingestion status is tracked from uploaded to processed/failed.
Unsupported or failed files surface actionable error states.
Start with common text/PDF/doc formats only.
Virus scanning hook should be left in the pipeline design.
Access scope metadata must be tenant-aware.

stories.title: Chunking, embeddings, and semantic retrieval

tasks.title: Chunking, embeddings, and semantic retrieval
Processed documents are chunked and embedded into `knowledge_chunks` using pgvector.
Semantic search can return top relevant chunks scoped to company and access policy.
Retrieval results include source document references for explainability.
Re-ingesting a document replaces or versions prior chunks safely.
Background worker should handle embedding generation asynchronously.
Keep embedding model/version metadata for future reindexing.
Retrieval must enforce company and scope filters before similarity ranking.

stories.title: Agent and company memory persistence

tasks.title: Agent and company memory persistence
Memory items can be stored as company-wide or agent-specific records.
Memory supports types such as preference, decision pattern, summary, role memory, and company memory.
Memory retrieval can filter by agent, recency, salience, and semantic relevance.
Users can delete or expire memory items according to policy controls.
Store summaries, not raw chain-of-thought.
Include validity windows for stale memory handling.
Deletion must respect tenant boundaries and future privacy controls.

stories.title: Grounded context retrieval service

tasks.title: Grounded context retrieval service
Retrieval service composes context from documents, memory, recent tasks, and relevant records.
Retrieval respects agent data scopes and human/company permissions.
Returned context is normalized into structured prompt-ready sections.
Retrieval source references are persisted for downstream audit/explanation.
Keep retrieval deterministic and testable.
Avoid direct prompt assembly in UI or controllers.
Cache low-risk repeated retrievals in Redis where appropriate.

epics.title: Task, workflow, and approval orchestration

stories.title: Task lifecycle and assignment

tasks.title: Task lifecycle and assignment
Users can create tasks with type, title, description, priority, due date, and assigned agent.
Tasks support statuses: new, in_progress, blocked, awaiting_approval, completed, failed.
Agent-created subtasks can reference a parent task.
Task detail stores input payload, output payload, rationale summary, and confidence score where available.
Task entity is the backbone for orchestration and audit.
Assignment must reject paused/archived agents.
Keep task APIs CQRS-lite.

stories.title: Workflow definitions, instances, and triggers

tasks.title: Workflow definitions, instances, and triggers
System supports workflow templates/definitions with versioned JSON definitions.
Workflow instances can be started manually, by schedule, or by internal event.
Instance state and current step are persisted and queryable.
Failed or blocked workflow steps surface exceptions for review.
Start with opinionated predefined workflows, not arbitrary builder UX.
Scheduler should use background workers with distributed locking.
Version workflow definitions to avoid breaking in-flight instances.

stories.title: Approval requests and decision chains

tasks.title: Approval requests and decision chains
Approval requests can be created for tasks, workflows, or actions with threshold context.
Approval can target a required role, specific user, or ordered multi-step chain.
Approve/reject decisions update the linked entity state and are auditable.
Expired/cancelled approvals are handled explicitly and do not execute actions.
v1 can support single-step and ordered multi-step chains.
Approval UX should show concise rationale and affected data.
Rejection should support comments for agent/user feedback.

stories.title: Escalations, retries, and long-running background execution

tasks.title: Escalations, retries, and long-running background execution
Background workers execute scheduled jobs, workflow progression, retries, and long-running tasks.
Retry policy distinguishes transient failures from permanent policy/business failures.
Blocked or failed executions create visible exceptions/escalations.
Outbox-backed side effects are dispatched reliably without duplication.
Use idempotency keys/correlation IDs for retries.
Redis can coordinate locks and ephemeral execution state.
Keep worker execution tenant-scoped.

epics.title: Agent communication and orchestration engine

stories.title: Direct chat with named agents

tasks.title: Direct chat with named agents
Users can open a direct conversation with an agent from roster or dashboard.
Messages are stored with sender type, sender ID, conversation type, and timestamps.
Agent responses use the selected agent’s persona, role brief, and scoped context.
Chat can create or link to tasks when the interaction becomes actionable.
Keep conversation history tenant-scoped and paginated.
Do not expose raw reasoning; return concise rationale summaries where relevant.
Support direct-agent channel type first.

stories.title: Shared orchestration pipeline for single-agent tasks

tasks.title: Shared orchestration pipeline for single-agent tasks
Orchestration resolves target agent, intent/task type, and runtime context.
Prompt builder composes role instructions, company context, memory, policies, and tool schemas.
Tool executor returns structured results only and records execution metadata.
Final response includes user-facing output plus structured task/audit artifacts.
Shared engine, distinct agent configs; no bespoke stacks per role.
Keep orchestration service separate from HTTP/UI concerns.
Persist correlation IDs across prompt, tool, task, and audit records.

stories.title: Policy-enforced tool execution

tasks.title: Policy-enforced tool execution
Tool execution requests are evaluated for tenant scope, action type, autonomy level, thresholds, and approval requirements.
Allowed executions are persisted in `tool_executions` with request/response and policy decision metadata.
Denied executions return a safe user-facing explanation and create audit records.
Internal tools can call domain modules through typed contracts rather than direct DB access.
Separate read/recommend/execute action types.
Never let the model call external systems directly.
Start with internal tool abstractions and a small connector set.

stories.title: Manager-worker multi-agent collaboration

tasks.title: Manager-worker multi-agent collaboration
A coordinator flow can decompose a request into explicit subtasks for multiple agents.
Subtasks are assigned, tracked, and linked to a parent task/workflow.
Final output consolidates sub-results into one response with source attribution by agent.
Collaboration is bounded to explicit plans; uncontrolled agent loops are prevented.
Use manager-worker pattern only, not free-form chatter.
Put limits on fan-out, depth, and runtime.
Consolidation should preserve rationale summaries per contributing agent.

stories.title: Daily briefings and executive summaries

tasks.title: Daily briefings and executive summaries
System can generate scheduled daily briefings and weekly summaries per company.
Briefings aggregate alerts, approvals, KPI highlights, anomalies, and notable agent updates.
Generated summaries are stored as messages/notifications and visible in the dashboard.
Users can configure delivery preferences for in-app and mobile notifications.
Start with scheduled in-app generation; email delivery can follow.
Summaries should reference underlying tasks/approvals where possible.
Use cached dashboard aggregates to reduce generation cost.

epics.title: Executive cockpit, auditability, and mobile companion

stories.title: Executive cockpit dashboard

tasks.title: Executive cockpit dashboard
Dashboard shows daily briefing, pending approvals, alerts, department KPI cards, and recent activity feed.
Users can drill into agents, tasks, workflows, and approvals from dashboard widgets.
Dashboard queries are tenant-scoped and performant for interactive use.
Empty states guide setup when no agents, workflows, or knowledge exist yet.
Web app is the primary command center.
Start with summary KPIs and simple trend indicators.
Use Redis caching for expensive aggregate queries.

stories.title: Audit trail and explainability views

tasks.title: Audit trail and explainability views
Important actions create business audit events with actor, action, target, outcome, rationale summary, and data sources used.
Users can view audit history filtered by agent, task, workflow, and date range.
Action detail shows linked approvals, tool executions, and affected entities where available.
Explanations are concise and operational, without exposing raw chain-of-thought.
Auditability is a domain feature, not just app logging.
Keep data source references human-readable.
Ensure audit views respect role-based access.

stories.title: Alerts, notifications, and approval inbox

tasks.title: Alerts, notifications, and approval inbox
System generates notifications for approvals, escalations, workflow failures, and briefing availability.
Users can view and act on pending approvals from a dedicated inbox.
Notification state supports unread/read and actioned statuses.
Background dispatcher delivers in-app notifications reliably.
Model notifications separately from messages if needed for UX.
Prioritize approval and exception alerts in sorting.
Keep notification fan-out out of request path via outbox.

stories.title: Mobile companion for approvals, alerts, and quick chat

tasks.title: Mobile companion for approvals, alerts, and quick chat
.NET MAUI app supports sign-in, company selection, alert list, approval actions, daily briefing view, and direct agent chat.
Mobile can display quick company status and task follow-up summaries.
Approval decisions made on mobile update the same backend approval state as web.
Mobile scope is intentionally limited and does not require full admin/workflow parity.
Responsive web may cover some early mobile needs, but MAUI app remains the target companion.
Reuse backend APIs; no mobile-specific business logic.
Optimize for concise payloads and intermittent connectivity.

epics.title: EP-7: Persistent agent operation and trigger system

stories.title: ST-701 - Scheduled agent triggers

tasks.title: Implement scheduled trigger data model and persistence schema
Build CRUD API endpoints for schedule trigger management with validation
Add cron parser and next-run calculator with timezone support

acceptanceCriteria: Users can create, update, enable, disable, and delete schedule-based triggers for an agent through a persisted API.
A trigger supports cron-like expressions with explicit timezone configuration and next-run time calculation.
When a scheduled trigger is enabled, the system enqueues exactly one execution request for each due schedule window.
Disabling a scheduled trigger prevents any new executions from being enqueued after the disable timestamp.
Invalid cron expressions or unsupported timezones are rejected with validation errors and no trigger is persisted.

stories.title: ST-702 - Event-driven triggers

tasks.title: Define event trigger schema and supported event type registry
Integrate trigger matching into platform event bus consumers
Implement idempotency handling for event-triggered execution requests

acceptanceCriteria: The system allows registration of triggers bound to supported platform event types including task created, task updated, document uploaded, and workflow state changed.
When a supported event is emitted, matching enabled triggers are evaluated within the same tenant boundary.
Trigger payloads include event metadata, source entity identifiers, tenant identifier, and correlation identifier.
Duplicate delivery of the same event does not create duplicate agent executions for the same trigger and event identifier.
Unsupported event types are rejected during trigger creation.

stories.title: ST-703 - Condition-based triggers

tasks.title: Implement condition expression model and validation rules
Build condition evaluator for metric and entity-field comparisons
Persist condition evaluation state to support edge-triggered firing

acceptanceCriteria: Users can define condition-based triggers using supported operators against named metrics or entity fields.
The evaluation engine can process threshold conditions such as greater than, less than, equals, and changed since last evaluation.
A condition trigger fires only when its condition transitions from false to true unless configured for repeated firing.
Condition evaluation results are persisted with evaluation timestamp, input values, and outcome.
Invalid condition definitions are rejected with field-level validation errors.

stories.title: ST-704 - Trigger evaluation engine and auditability

tasks.title: Implement background worker for trigger evaluation and execution dispatch
Add policy-aware orchestration entry point for trigger-initiated runs
Create audit event writer for trigger evaluations and executions
Add retry and dead-letter handling for failed trigger execution jobs

acceptanceCriteria: A background worker evaluates due scheduled triggers and pending condition checks on a recurring polling interval.
The evaluation engine records an idempotency key per trigger execution attempt and prevents duplicate orchestration starts.
All trigger executions produce audit records containing trigger id, trigger type, agent id, tenant id, execution status, and correlation id.
Policy checks are executed before orchestration start, and blocked executions are recorded with denial reason.
Worker failures are retried according to configured retry policy and failed attempts are visible in audit records.

epics.title: EPIC-1 — Proactive agent behavior and initiative

stories.title: ST-A201 — Agent-initiated task creation

tasks.title: Implement proactive task creation service with trigger-to-task mapping
Add idempotency and deduplication checks for agent-created tasks
Persist task provenance and audit metadata for proactive actions

acceptanceCriteria: Given a configured trigger or analysis result, the system creates a task without user prompting and persists the originating agent, trigger source, and creation reason.
Created tasks include required fields: title, description, priority, status, assignee or queue, sourceType='agent', and correlationId.
Duplicate task creation is prevented for the same trigger event and correlationId within the configured deduplication window.
Each agent-initiated task creation is recorded in the audit log with tenantId, agentId, timestamp, and payload diff.

stories.title: ST-A202 — Alert generation framework

tasks.title: Create alert domain model, persistence schema, and CRUD APIs
Implement alert classification and fingerprint-based deduplication pipeline
Add alert query filters and pagination to alert retrieval endpoints

acceptanceCriteria: The system generates alerts from agent detections with required fields: type, severity, title, summary, evidence, status, tenantId, and correlationId.
Alerts are categorized into at least risk, anomaly, and opportunity types, and into at least low, medium, high, and critical severities.
Alert generation applies deduplication so repeated detections with the same fingerprint do not create multiple open alerts.
Users can query alerts by tenant, type, severity, status, and createdAt through an API returning paginated results.

stories.title: ST-A203 — Escalation policies

tasks.title: Implement policy evaluation engine for threshold- and rule-based escalations
Add escalation state tracking to prevent repeated level triggers
Expose escalation records and policy evaluation history via API

acceptanceCriteria: The system evaluates escalation policies against alert and task events using configurable thresholds, timers, and rule conditions.
When an escalation condition is met, the system creates an escalation record with policyId, sourceEntityId, escalationLevel, reason, and triggeredAt.
Escalations are executed only once per policy level for the same source entity unless the entity is resolved and re-opened.
Policy evaluation results and escalation actions are written to the audit log and are traceable by correlationId.

stories.title: ST-A204 — Proactive messaging with guardrailed autonomy

tasks.title: Implement proactive message generation and delivery workflow for inbox and notification channels
Integrate autonomy and policy guardrail checks into proactive action execution
Persist message delivery records and policy decision outcomes

acceptanceCriteria: When a proactive task, alert, or escalation is created, the system can generate a user-facing message or briefing linked to the originating entity.
Before sending, the system enforces autonomy level and policy guardrails; blocked actions are not delivered and return a policy decision reason.
Delivered proactive messages include channel, recipient, subject or title, body, sourceEntityId, and sentAt, and are persisted for retrieval.
Integration tests verify that allowed actions are delivered and disallowed actions are blocked for at least two distinct autonomy levels.

epics.title: EPIC-2 — Strong agent identity and behavioral consistency

stories.title: ST-A301 — Communication style enforcement across response channels

tasks.title: Add agent communication profile schema for tone, persona, and style constraints
Inject communication profile into chat, task output, and document generation prompt builders
Implement response style validation rules and automated tests for forbidden tone patterns

acceptanceCriteria: Given an agent with configured tone, persona, and communication rules, when a chat response, task summary, or generated output is produced, then the prompt payload includes the agent identity profile fields before model invocation.
Responses generated for the same agent across chat, task, and document output paths include the configured style directives and pass automated rule checks for forbidden tone violations.
When an agent configuration is updated, subsequent generations use the new style settings without requiring service restart.
If no explicit identity profile exists for an agent, the system applies a default fallback profile and records the fallback usage in logs.

stories.title: ST-A302 — Role boundary enforcement and delegation behavior

tasks.title: Create role responsibility policy model with allowed domains, denied domains, and delegation targets
Implement pre-response scope evaluation middleware for agent requests
Add delegation response templates and escalation action wiring for out-of-scope requests
Write integration tests covering in-scope processing and out-of-scope delegation paths

acceptanceCriteria: Given an agent with defined domain responsibilities, when a request falls outside its allowed scope, then the agent response includes a delegation or escalation action instead of a direct answer.
Requests classified as in-scope are processed normally and do not trigger boundary violation events.
Boundary decisions are evaluated against configured allow/deny responsibility rules with deterministic test coverage for at least one in-scope and one out-of-scope scenario per agent role.
Out-of-scope handling events are persisted with agent id, requested domain, matched rule, and delegation target.

stories.title: ST-A303 — Identity-driven prompt shaping with role, seniority, and behavior patterns

tasks.title: Extend prompt builder to compose structured identity sections from agent and tenant configuration
Implement instruction precedence rules for tenant policy, agent identity, and task context
Add non-production prompt preview endpoint with redaction for sensitive fields

acceptanceCriteria: Prompt assembly includes agent role, seniority, business responsibility, collaboration norms, and personality traits in a structured identity section.
The prompt builder produces different prompt payloads for agents with different identity configurations while preserving shared system safety instructions.
Prompt preview or debug output exposes the resolved identity section for internal inspection in non-production environments.
Unit tests verify prompt composition precedence when tenant policy, agent identity, and task context all provide overlapping instructions.

stories.title: ST-A305 — Identity audit signals for traceability and consistency monitoring

tasks.title: Extend audit event schema to capture agent identity and behavior decision metadata
Emit audit events from generation pipeline and boundary enforcement middleware
Build internal audit query API for filtering by agent and time range

acceptanceCriteria: Each agent generation event stores identity metadata including agent name, role, responsibility domain, prompt profile version, and boundary decision outcome.
Audit records can be queried by agent id and time range through an internal API endpoint.
When fallback identity configuration or boundary delegation is used, the audit event includes a machine-readable reason code.
Automated tests verify audit event creation for normal generation, fallback identity usage, and out-of-scope delegation scenarios.

epics.title: EPIC-3 — Executive cockpit as a real control room

stories.title: Department-based executive dashboard composition

tasks.title: Implement department dashboard configuration schema and persistence
Build dashboard composition API for department sections and widgets
Render server-driven department layout in executive cockpit UI
Add authorization checks for department and widget visibility

acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard API returns department sections for at least finance, sales, support, and operations in a deterministic display order.
Each department section includes configured widgets, summary counts, and navigation metadata required by the frontend.
Users only receive department sections and widgets permitted by their role and tenant scope.
Frontend renders department sections from server-driven configuration without hardcoded department-specific layouts.
Automated tests verify section ordering, role-based visibility, and fallback behavior when a department has no data.

stories.title: Agent status cards with workload, health, and recent activity

tasks.title: Create agent status aggregation service for workload, health, and alerts
Expose agent status card endpoint with recent activity payload
Implement agent status card components and detail navigation

acceptanceCriteria: Each agent card displays current workload, health status, active alerts count, and the 5 most recent actions.
Agent status values are derived from live system data and refresh within 60 seconds of source updates.
Selecting an agent card opens a detail view or deep link with the agent's active tasks, workflows, and alerts.
Health status is computed using defined thresholds for failed runs, stalled work, and policy violations.
Automated tests validate status calculation, recent action ordering, and deep-link routing.

stories.title: KPI and anomaly surfacing across departments

tasks.title: Implement KPI query layer and baseline comparison calculations
Build anomaly detection rules engine for dashboard metrics
Add KPI tiles and anomaly list components with interactive filters

acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard shows configured KPI tiles per department with current value, comparison baseline, and trend direction.
Anomalies are flagged when metric values breach configured thresholds or deviate from baseline by configured percentages.
Each anomaly includes severity, impacted department, timestamp, and a link to the underlying metric or workflow context.
KPI and anomaly data can be filtered by time range and department without full page reload.
Automated tests verify anomaly detection logic, severity mapping, and filter behavior.

stories.title: Action-oriented insights and deep links to operational work

tasks.title: Implement insight scoring and prioritization service for actionable items
Create unified deep-link resolver for tasks, workflows, and approvals
Build action queue UI with acknowledgment interactions
Persist per-user insight acknowledgment state

acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard displays a prioritized action queue containing approvals, risks, blocked workflows, and opportunities.
Each action item includes priority, reason, owner, due time or SLA state, and a deep link to the target task, workflow, or approval.
Priority ordering is consistent with configured scoring rules and remains stable for identical scores.
Users can mark an insight as acknowledged, and the acknowledgment state persists across refreshes for that user.
Automated tests verify action scoring, deep-link generation, and acknowledgment persistence.

stories.title: Dashboard performance, caching, and refresh behavior

tasks.title: Add cache layer for dashboard aggregates and widget payloads
Implement event-driven cache invalidation for cockpit data sources
Enable partial widget refresh and loading states in cockpit UI
Create performance test suite and telemetry for cockpit endpoints

acceptanceCriteria: Executive cockpit initial load completes in under 2.5 seconds at p95 for cached requests in the staging performance environment.
Dashboard data endpoints use cache keys scoped by tenant, user role, department filters, and time range.
Cache invalidation occurs within 60 seconds after relevant task, workflow, approval, or agent status updates.
Frontend supports partial widget refresh without reloading the full dashboard shell.
Performance tests and observability dashboards report endpoint latency, cache hit rate, and widget render timing.

epics.title: EPIC-4 — Continuous company narrative and event-enriched briefings

stories.title: Build event-driven briefing generation pipeline

tasks.title: Implement briefing update job producer for supported domain events
Add idempotency, retry, and failure tracking to briefing generation jobs
Wire scheduled daily and weekly briefing triggers into the unified pipeline

acceptanceCriteria: When a supported business event is emitted, a briefing update job is created within 30 seconds with tenant, company, event type, and correlation identifiers persisted.
Supported event types include task status changes, workflow state changes, approval requests, approval decisions, escalations, and agent-generated alerts.
Duplicate events with the same idempotency key do not create more than one briefing update job.
If briefing generation fails, the job is retried according to configured retry policy and the final failure state is recorded with error details.
Scheduled daily and weekly briefing jobs continue to run and use the same generation pipeline as event-driven jobs.

stories.title: Aggregate cross-agent insights into unified briefing sections

tasks.title: Create cross-agent insight aggregation service with grouping and conflict detection
Extend briefing data model to persist agent contribution metadata and source references
Expose narrative and structured briefing sections through briefing read APIs

acceptanceCriteria: A generated briefing can include contributions from multiple agents and each contribution is stored with agent identifier, source reference, timestamp, and confidence metadata.
The aggregation service groups related insights into a single section when they share the same company entity, workflow, task, or event correlation identifier.
If two agents provide conflicting assessments for the same topic, the briefing output marks the section as conflicting and includes both viewpoints.
The generated briefing payload exposes both narrative text and structured sections in the API response.
Aggregation excludes agent contributions that are outside the configured tenant and company scope.

stories.title: Link briefing content to tasks, approvals, workflows, and priorities

tasks.title: Implement priority scoring and section ordering for briefing content
Add entity linking from briefing sections to tasks, approvals, and workflows
Populate structured briefing metrics for alerts, approvals, blocked workflows, and overdue tasks

acceptanceCriteria: Each briefing section referencing operational work includes resolvable links or identifiers to the related task, workflow instance, or approval record.
Briefing sections are ordered by priority so that alerts, risks, and escalations appear before informational updates.
Priority is derived from persisted severity rules and can be validated from the structured output without parsing narrative text.
If a linked task, workflow, or approval is deleted or inaccessible, the briefing API returns a stable placeholder state instead of failing.
The structured output includes counts for critical alerts, open approvals, blocked workflows, and overdue tasks.

stories.title: Support personalized briefing preferences and delivery controls

tasks.title: Create persistence and APIs for user briefing preferences and tenant defaults
Apply preference-based filtering during briefing assembly
Add request validation and error handling for invalid preference payloads

acceptanceCriteria: Users can save briefing preferences for delivery frequency, included focus areas, and priority threshold through authenticated API endpoints.
Generated briefings respect the saved user preferences by filtering out excluded focus areas and sections below the configured priority threshold.
If no user preference exists, the system applies tenant-level defaults and records which defaults were used.
Preference changes affect the next generated briefing without requiring service restart or manual cache invalidation.
API validation rejects unsupported focus areas and invalid delivery frequencies with 4xx responses and descriptive error codes.

epics.title: EPIC-5 — Agent activity timeline and transparency layer

stories.title: Unified real-time agent activity feed

tasks.title: Implement tenant-scoped activity event store and feed query API
Publish agent activity events to real-time subscription channel
Build chronological activity feed UI with live updates and pagination

acceptanceCriteria: A tenant-scoped activity feed endpoint returns agent activity events in reverse chronological order with cursor-based pagination.
New agent activity events are delivered to connected clients within 2 seconds of event persistence via a real-time subscription channel.
Each activity event includes eventId, tenantId, agentId, eventType, occurredAt, status, summary, correlationId, and source metadata.
The feed excludes events from other tenants and returns HTTP 403 for unauthorized access attempts.
When no events exist for the selected scope, the API returns an empty result set with a valid next cursor value of null.

stories.title: Correlated action timeline across tasks, workflows, approvals, and tools

tasks.title: Add correlation model and entity link resolution service for activity events
Expose correlated timeline API for tasks workflows approvals and tool executions
Implement drill-down correlation panel in the activity timeline UI

acceptanceCriteria: Activity events with the same correlationId are grouped into a single timeline view showing related task, workflow, approval, and tool execution records.
Selecting an activity item displays linked entities and their current statuses without requiring a full page reload.
If a linked entity is missing or deleted, the timeline renders a visible unavailable state without breaking the rest of the correlation view.
Correlation queries for a single activity item return results in under 1 second for datasets up to 10,000 related events in performance test fixtures.
Correlation links are restricted to entities within the same tenant and respect existing authorization rules for tasks, workflows, approvals, and tools.

stories.title: Human-readable activity summaries and transparency metadata

tasks.title: Create event-to-summary formatter library for supported activity types
Attach normalized summary payloads to feed and correlation responses
Add fixture-based tests for summary generation across representative event types

acceptanceCriteria: Each supported event type is transformed into a human-readable summary using a deterministic template or formatter before being returned to clients.
Summaries include actor, action, target, and outcome fields when source data is available, and omit missing fields without rendering placeholder text such as 'undefined' or 'null'.
The API returns both raw technical payload and normalized summary payload for each activity event.
For at least 20 predefined event types, automated tests verify the generated summary text matches expected output fixtures.
Summary generation completes during event ingestion or read-time enrichment without increasing median feed response time above 300 ms in benchmark tests.

stories.title: Filtering, drill-down, and audit deep linking

tasks.title: Implement server-side filtering and indexed queries for activity feed dimensions
Build filter controls and URL state synchronization in the timeline UI
Add activity detail drawer with audit deep-link handling
Write integration tests for filter combinations and audit navigation

acceptanceCriteria: Users can filter the activity feed by agent, department, task, event type, status, and timeframe, and the returned results match all selected filters.
Applying or clearing filters updates the URL query string so the current view can be reloaded or shared without losing state.

epics.title: EPIC-21 — Tenant-scoped finance simulation engine and deterministic time progression

stories.title: Build tenant-scoped simulation state service and progression runner

tasks.title: Implement simulation state persistence model and tenant/company-scoped repository

acceptanceCriteria: A tenant/company simulation state can be created, read, updated, paused, resumed, and stopped through additive backend APIs without changing existing finance APIs.
When simulation status is running, the simulated company date advances by exactly 1 day every 10 seconds based on server time.
When simulation status is paused, simulated time does not advance and the last simulated date is preserved.
When simulation status is stopped, the active simulation session is ended, no further progression occurs, and a new start creates a clean session state.
Simulation state is isolated by tenant and company so one company's progression does not affect another company's state.
Given the same seed, start date, and configuration, two runs produce the same sequence of simulated dates and scheduled generation decisions.
The service stores and exposes current status, current simulated date/time, last progression timestamp, generation enabled flag, seed, and active session identifier.

stories.title: Generate deterministic finance activity and anomaly scenarios into existing finance workflows

tasks.title: Implement finance generation policy that maps simulated day progression to realistic invoice, bill, transaction, balance, and recurring expense outputs
Integrate generated records with existing finance workflow, approval, payment, and history services
Build deterministic anomaly injection scheduler and scenario factory using seeded generation rules
Add end-to-end tests validating generated scenarios appear in existing finance pages and workflows

acceptanceCriteria: When generation is enabled and a simulated day advances, the system creates or updates finance records for the active company in existing finance tables/services rather than a mock-only store.

Generated daily activity includes invoices, bills, transactions, balances, recurring expense instances, payment status changes, workflow tasks, approval requests where required, and finance history events.

Across a deterministic run, the generated invoice set includes pending invoice over approval threshold, different approval currency, partial payment, full or over-payment, due soon, overdue, and normal low-risk pending scenarios.

Across a deterministic run, the generated policy scenarios include amount just below threshold, exactly at threshold, just above threshold, requiring human approval, eligible without escalation, and already approved or no longer actionable.

Anomalies are injected periodically rather than every simulated day and include duplicate vendor charge, unusually high amount, category mismatch, missing document, suspicious payment timing, multiple payments, and payment before expected state transition.
Generated anomalies and review signals are visible through existing finance review, approval, anomaly, and history workflows for the active company.
Given the same seed and configuration, repeated runs generate the same finance records, anomaly days, and workflow outcomes for the same simulated dates.

stories.title: Expose finance page simulation controls and live simulation status

tasks.title: Add finance top-panel simulation controls and generation toggle bound to company simulation APIs
Render live simulation status, simulated date/time, and last progression metadata in the finance page
Implement optional step-forward and refresh actions with optimistic UI state handling

acceptanceCriteria: When the feature is enabled, the finance page top panel displays Start, Pause, and Stop controls for the active company simulation.
The finance page displays a simulation status indicator with values stopped, running, or paused and updates within 2 seconds of a control action.
The finance page displays the current simulated date/time, last progression timestamp, and whether generation is enabled.
A user can toggle Generate financial data during simulation between On and Off before starting and while paused, and the selected value is persisted to simulation state.
If Step forward 1 day is implemented, invoking it advances the simulated date by exactly 1 day once and triggers one day of generation only when generation is On.
If Refresh simulation state is implemented, it reloads the latest backend state without requiring a full page refresh.
Generated records become visible in existing finance lists and detail views after progression without introducing a separate simulation-only UI.

stories.title: Add feature gating, observability, and safe-disable behavior for simulation

tasks.title: Implement environment-aware feature flags for simulation UI visibility and backend execution gates
Add backend guards to block simulation routes, jobs, and manual triggers when execution is disabled
Persist simulation event logs, run history, anomaly reasons, and generation errors for operator review
Expose recent simulation history and error state in sandbox admin or finance diagnostics view

acceptanceCriteria: A configuration-driven feature flag can disable simulation UI visibility and backend execution independently.
When UI visibility is disabled, simulation controls and related simulation status panels are not rendered anywhere in the app.
When backend execution is disabled, simulation control APIs and admin actions return a safe disabled response and no simulation session can start.
When simulation is fully disabled, no background progression or finance generation jobs run for any tenant/company.
The system records simulation run history including session identifier, status transitions, generated record counts per simulated day, injected anomalies, warnings, and errors.
Operators can retrieve recent simulation history and per-day generation logs for a tenant/company from an existing admin or finance-facing view.
Disabling simulation does not break standard finance page loading, finance workflows, or existing finance APIs.

epics.title: EPIC-22 — Action-Centric Executive Dashboard Redesign

stories.title: Implement Today’s Focus aggregation API and primary decision panel

tasks.title: Create FocusItem model and implement IFocusEngine with cross-domain scoring and normalization
Add GET /api/dashboard/focus endpoint with tenant and user scoping
Build TodayFocusPanel.razor to render 3–5 prioritized action cards with CTA navigation
Add integration and component tests for focus ordering, field completeness, and navigation behavior

acceptanceCriteria: GET /api/dashboard/focus returns 3 to 5 FocusItem records for a valid companyId and userId, ordered by PriorityScore descending.
Each FocusItem response includes non-empty Id, Title, Description, ActionType, PriorityScore, and NavigationTarget fields.
PriorityScore is normalized to an integer between 0 and 100 for every returned item.
The focus aggregation includes items sourced from approvals, tasks, anomalies, and finance alerts when data exists in those domains.
TodayFocusPanel renders returned focus items as cards with title, short description, and CTA button.
Clicking a focus item CTA navigates to the exact NavigationTarget returned by the API.

stories.title: Refactor dashboard action queue into inline prioritized action list

tasks.title: Extend queue service with GetTopActions and paginated GetAllActions using priority, due time, and impact sorting
Remove legacy right sidebar queue from Dashboard.razor and insert TopActionsList below Today’s Focus
Implement TopActionsList.razor with top 5 rendering and full queue navigation

acceptanceCriteria: The dashboard no longer renders the legacy right sidebar action queue component.
TopActionsList renders exactly the top 5 actions inline below Today’s Focus when 5 or more actions exist.
The action service returns actions sorted by priority descending, then due time ascending, then impact score descending.
The dashboard provides a visible 'View full queue' link that navigates to the dedicated queue page.
The full queue endpoint supports pagination parameters and returns deterministic ordering across pages.

stories.title: Transform KPI and finance sections into decision-support signals and snapshots

tasks.title: Implement BusinessSignal model and ISignalEngine rules for operational load and approval bottleneck detection
Add finance snapshot computation service and GET /api/dashboard/finance-snapshot endpoint
Replace KPI cards with BusinessSignalsPanel.razor and add FinanceSnapshotCard.razor with missing-data CTA state
Add automated tests for signal rule evaluation and finance snapshot calculations

acceptanceCriteria: ISignalEngine.GenerateSignals(companyId) returns at least one BusinessSignal when threshold rules are met for seeded operational data.
BusinessSignalsPanel replaces legacy KPI cards and renders each signal with severity-specific color and icon treatment.
GET /api/dashboard/finance-snapshot returns Cash, BurnRate, RunwayDays, and RiskLevel for tenants with finance data.
BurnRate is computed from the average of the last 30 days of expenses and RunwayDays is derived from Cash divided by BurnRate when BurnRate is greater than zero.
FinanceSnapshotCard displays a 'Connect accounting' CTA when finance data is missing or incomplete.
FinanceSnapshotCard displays cash, runway days, and risk badge when finance data is available.

stories.title: Rebuild dashboard layout, deep links, and telemetry for action-first usage

tasks.title: Refactor Dashboard.razor layout hierarchy and responsive grid styling for the new section order
Implement deep-link routing for approvals, tasks, and finance actions with contextual query parameters
Add DashboardInteractionService to capture focus clicks, time-to-first-action, and scroll depth events
Simplify DepartmentCard rendering to high-signal summaries and hide zero-value metrics

acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard.razor renders sections in this order: CompanyHealthBanner, TodayFocusPanel, TopActionsList, FinanceSnapshot and OperationsSnapshot grid, AgentActivityPanel, DepartmentCards.
The dashboard uses a responsive two-column layout on desktop and collapses to a single column on smaller viewports.
All dashboard action CTAs route to valid deep links including required query parameters for context, such as /approvals?filter=pending.
Telemetry records focus item clicks, first action timestamp, and scroll depth for each dashboard session.
A dashboard session emits a measurable time-to-first-action metric when a user clicks any actionable CTA.
Department cards render no more than 3 key signals and suppress zero-value metrics.

epics.title: EPIC-23 — Financial Statements and Period-End Reporting from Ledger

stories.title: Map ledger accounts to financial statement sections and reporting classifications

tasks.title: Create financial statement mapping entities, enums, and EF Core configurations
Implement company-scoped APIs for account mapping CRUD and validation
Add database migration, uniqueness constraints, and bootstrap defaults for seeded charts of accounts

acceptanceCriteria: A financial statement mapping model exists that links each active company account to a report section and line classification for Balance Sheet, Profit & Loss, and Cash Flow preparation.
The system prevents an account from being left unmapped when the account is active and marked as reportable.
API endpoints allow listing, creating, and updating account-to-statement mappings scoped by company.
A validation endpoint returns all unmapped or conflicting accounts for a company with deterministic error codes.
Database constraints enforce uniqueness for one active mapping per company account and statement type.

stories.title: Generate Profit & Loss and Balance Sheet reports by fiscal period

tasks.title: Implement ledger aggregation services for period-based P&L and Balance Sheet generation
Expose read APIs for Profit & Loss and Balance Sheet with period and company filters
Add integration tests covering posted, unposted, and cross-period journal entry scenarios

acceptanceCriteria: Given a company and fiscal period, the Profit & Loss API returns line items, totals, and net income derived from posted journal entries only.
Given a company and fiscal period, the Balance Sheet API returns assets, liabilities, equity, and balancing totals derived from posted journal entries only.
Closed periods return stable report values that match the corresponding trial balance snapshot totals for the same period.
Report generation excludes unposted journal entries and entries dated outside the requested fiscal period.
Automated tests verify that Balance Sheet total assets equals total liabilities plus equity for seeded reporting scenarios.

stories.title: Support period-end close checks and reporting lock controls

tasks.title: Implement period-end validation service for reporting readiness checks
Add reporting lock state, audit fields, and authorization checks to period reporting workflows
Create migration and API endpoints for close checks, lock, and unlock operations

acceptanceCriteria: A period close validation endpoint returns blocking issues when the period contains unposted source documents, unbalanced journal entries, or missing statement mappings.
A reporting lock can be applied to a closed fiscal period and prevents regeneration of stored statement outputs unless an authorized unlock operation is performed.
The system records who executed close validation, lock, and unlock operations with timestamps in the audit trail.
Attempting to regenerate locked period reports returns a consistent authorization or state error response.
Automated tests verify that lock state changes are enforced across API and background job execution paths.

stories.title: Persist report snapshots and provide statement drilldown APIs

tasks.title: Create financial statement snapshot tables and versioning model
Implement snapshot generation job and persistence workflow for financial statements
Build report line drilldown queries from statement lines to journal entries and lines

acceptanceCriteria: The system can persist versioned Profit & Loss and Balance Sheet snapshots for a company and fiscal period after successful generation.
Snapshot records include generation timestamp, source period, version number, and hash or checksum of included balances.
A drilldown API returns the journal entries and journal lines contributing to a selected report line for a given snapshot or live report.
Regenerating an unlocked period creates a new snapshot version without overwriting prior versions.
Automated tests verify that drilldown totals reconcile to the selected report line amount within the same company and period.

epics.title: EPIC-24 — Cash Flow Operations, Payment Settlement, and Bank Reconciliation

stories.title: Implement finance counterparty master data for customers and suppliers

tasks.title: Extend customer and supplier schemas with finance master data fields and account mappings
Create migration and backfill scripts for counterparty finance fields and lookup indexes
Add admin APIs and validation for customer and supplier finance data management
Build admin UI screens for viewing and editing finance counterparty details

acceptanceCriteria: Customer and Supplier persistence models include paymentTerms, taxId, creditLimit, preferredPaymentMethod, and defaultAccountMapping fields.
A database migration adds the new finance fields and creates indexes on tenant/company identifier plus name or reference columns.
Existing invoices and bills continue to resolve their linked customer or supplier records after migration in automated integration tests.
Admin API endpoints support create, update, get, and list operations for customers and suppliers with the new finance fields.
Admin UI forms display and persist all finance-relevant customer and supplier fields without validation errors for valid payloads.
Backfill logic populates default values for existing mock counterparties and completes successfully for seeded tenants.

stories.title: Implement payment records for incoming and outgoing cash movements

tasks.title: Create payment domain model, repository, and persistence constraints for cash movement records
Add payment create and list APIs with tenant scoping and request validation
Implement payment detail and list views in the finance workspace
Add migration and seed generation for payment tables and indexes

acceptanceCriteria: A Payment entity exists with paymentType, amount, currency, paymentDate, method, status, and counterpartyReference fields.
Database constraints reject payment records with amount less than or equal to zero and invalid paymentType values.
Payment APIs support creation and listing of both incoming and outgoing payments scoped to a tenant/company.
Payment inspection UI shows payment header fields, status, amount, currency, and linked counterparty reference.
A migration creates payment storage and indexes on tenant/company identifier, status, and paymentDate.
Seed/bootstrap logic generates realistic payment records for existing companies and completes without duplicate key errors.

stories.title: Implement payment allocation and document settlement status updates

tasks.title: Create payment allocation schema, foreign keys, and over-allocation validation rules
Implement settlement service to recalculate invoice and bill payment statuses from allocations
Add allocation APIs for creating and inspecting payment-to-document links
Write integration and edge-case tests for partial payments and status transitions

acceptanceCriteria: A PaymentAllocation entity supports linking one payment to multiple invoices or bills and multiple payments to a single invoice or bill.
Allocation validation prevents total allocated amount from exceeding the payment amount or the remaining open amount on an invoice or bill.
Partial allocations are supported and persisted with exact allocated amounts and currency-consistent validation.
Invoice and bill statuses automatically transition to unpaid, partially paid, or paid based on current allocation totals.
Automated tests cover over-allocation rejection, partial payment handling, multi-document allocation, and status recalculation after allocation changes.
Backfill logic can infer and generate allocations for existing mocked paid invoices and bills where source payments are available or synthesized.

stories.title: Implement bank transaction ingestion, reconciliation workflow, and cash ledger integration

tasks.title: Create bank transaction model, migration, indexes, and tenant bank account mapping
Build mock bank transaction importer and seeded bootstrap generation for existing companies
Implement reconciliation service linking bank transactions to payments with status updates
Integrate reconciled cash events with ledger posting and add idempotency coverage

acceptanceCriteria: A BankTransaction entity exists with bankAccount, bookingDate, valueDate, amount, referenceText, counterparty, and status fields.
Database migration creates bank transaction storage with indexes on tenant/company identifier, bankAccount, bookingDate, and amount.
Mock bank transaction import or bootstrap creates realistic transactions for onboarded companies and maps bank accounts to tenant finance settings.
Bank transaction APIs support list and detail retrieval with filtering by bank account, date range, status, and amount.
A reconciliation process can link a bank transaction to one or more payments and marks the transaction as unreconciled, partially reconciled, or reconciled.
Reconciling a bank transaction posts or links the corresponding cash ledger event exactly once, verified by automated idempotency tests.

epics.title: EPIC-25 — Reconciliation Engine, Match Suggestions, and Decision Persistence

stories.title: Build reconciliation matching engine for bank transactions, payments, invoices, and bills

tasks.title: Implement reconciliation scoring service with amount, date, reference, and counterparty rules
Add tenant-scoped reconciliation configuration for tolerances and scoring weights
Create automated test suite for ranked match generation across supported entity types

acceptanceCriteria: Given a bank transaction and candidate payment records, the engine returns ranked payment-to-bank matches using amount exact/near match, date proximity, reference similarity, and counterparty similarity.
Given an invoice or bill and candidate payment records, the engine returns ranked linkage suggestions for invoice-to-payment and bill-to-payment scenarios.
The engine computes a normalized confidence score between 0.00 and 1.00 for every suggestion and includes per-rule scoring details in the result payload.
Near-match amount tolerance and date proximity windows are configurable per tenant and applied during scoring.
Automated tests cover exact match, near amount match, date-only match, reference-only match, counterparty-only match, and no-match scenarios.

stories.title: Persist reconciliation suggestions and accepted reconciliation outcomes

tasks.title: Design and apply database schema for reconciliation suggestions and reconciliation results
Implement repository and service methods for suggestion lifecycle and reconciliation persistence
Add audit metadata and tenant isolation checks to reconciliation data access paths

acceptanceCriteria: A reconciliation suggestion record is stored with source entity IDs, target entity IDs, match type, confidence score, rule breakdown, status, and timestamps.
When a suggestion is accepted, the system persists a reconciliation result linking the relevant records and marks superseded suggestions as no longer actionable.
When a suggestion is rejected, the system updates the suggestion status to rejected and excludes it from default open suggestion queries.
Reconciliation records are tenant-scoped and auditable, with created-by and updated-by metadata persisted for all state changes.
Database tests verify create, accept, reject, and query behavior for payment-to-bank, invoice-to-payment, and bill-to-payment records.

stories.title: Expose reconciliation suggestion review and accept/reject workflow APIs

tasks.title: Implement reconciliation suggestion query endpoints with filtering and pagination
Implement accept and reject workflow endpoints with validation and state transition handling
Add integration tests for reconciliation workflow API behavior and error cases

acceptanceCriteria: An API endpoint returns open reconciliation suggestions filtered by tenant, entity type, status, and minimum confidence.
An API endpoint accepts a reconciliation suggestion and returns the persisted reconciliation result with linked record identifiers.
An API endpoint rejects a reconciliation suggestion and returns the updated suggestion status.
Accepting an already accepted or rejected suggestion returns a deterministic validation error response.
API integration tests verify authorization, validation, idempotency behavior, and response payloads for accept and reject actions.

epics.title: EPIC-26 — Cash Event Ledger Posting, Cash Position Analytics, and Upgrade Backfills

stories.title: Implement ledger posting for incoming and outgoing cash events with source traceability

tasks.title: Implement cash settlement posting service for AR and AP journal generation
Add source traceability mappings between payments, bank transactions, and journal entries
Add idempotency guards and replay-safe posting logic for cash events
Write automated tests for settlement posting and duplicate replay scenarios

acceptanceCriteria: When a customer payment is marked settled, the system creates exactly one balanced journal entry debiting cash and crediting accounts receivable for the settled amount.
When a supplier payment is marked settled, the system creates exactly one balanced journal entry crediting cash and debiting accounts payable for the settled amount.
Each journal entry created from a payment or bank event stores a traceable source reference including source type, source id, company id, and posting timestamp.
Reprocessing the same settled payment or bank event does not create duplicate journal entries and returns the existing posting result.
Automated tests cover incoming payment posting, outgoing payment posting, partial settlement handling, and idempotent replay behavior.

stories.title: Handle unmatched bank transactions and complete migration and backfill support for cash posting

tasks.title: Create schema migration for cash posting linkage fields and traceability tables
Implement backfill routine for existing payments, bank transactions, and posting state
Add unmatched bank transaction classification state and posting exclusion rules

acceptanceCriteria: A database migration adds all linkage fields and mapping tables required to connect payments, bank transactions, and journal entries.
A backfill job initializes required traceability and unmatched-state records for existing tenant finance data without violating foreign key constraints.
Unmatched bank transactions are persisted with an explicit unmatched status and do not generate AR or AP settlement journal entries until matched or manually classified.
Migration and backfill execution completes successfully against an existing onboarded company dataset and produces no duplicate posting links.
Operational logs record counts of migrated records, backfilled records, skipped records, and posting conflicts per company.

stories.title: Deliver cash position and cash flow APIs with finance dashboard widgets

tasks.title: Implement cash position and cash flow summary API endpoints
Build finance dashboard widgets for cash balance and receivable/payable views
Add integration tests for cash metrics against seeded ledger and payment scenarios

acceptanceCriteria: The API exposes tenant-scoped endpoints for current cash balance, expected incoming cash, expected outgoing cash, overdue receivables, and upcoming payables.
Current cash balance equals the sum of posted cash-account ledger movements for the selected company and date context.
Expected incoming and outgoing cash metrics are computed from open receivables, open payables, and scheduled payment data using documented query rules.
Finance dashboard widgets render all five cash metrics and refresh successfully from live API responses.
API and UI tests verify correct metric values for seeded scenarios including overdue invoices, upcoming bills, and recent cash movement.

stories.title: Add agent query support and performance strategy for cash analytics

tasks.title: Implement finance agent query resolvers for cash planning, overdue customers, and cash movement explanations
Add summary table migrations and aggregate backfill jobs if query performance requires pre-aggregation
Document live versus pre-aggregated cash metrics and validate query latency on seeded datasets

acceptanceCriteria: Agent query handlers return deterministic responses for 'what should I pay this week', 'which customers are overdue', and 'why is cash down this month' using tenant-scoped finance data.
For each supported agent query, the response includes the underlying record ids or metric components used to produce the answer.
If summary or projection tables are introduced, migrations and background backfill jobs are implemented and can rebuild aggregates for an existing company.
System documentation identifies which cash metrics are computed live versus pre-aggregated and includes refresh behavior for any cached or summarized data.
Performance tests demonstrate that dashboard and agent cash queries complete within agreed service thresholds on seeded multi-company datasets.

epics.title: EPIC-27 — Operational Finance Planning, Approval State, and Variance Insights

stories.title: Implement finance approval workflow state and pending approval APIs

tasks.title: Add ApprovalTask entity, workflow enums, and EF Core migration with tenant-scoped indexes
Implement approval rule evaluation and automatic task creation for bills, payments, and exceptions
Expose pending approvals API and approval action endpoints for approve, reject, and escalate
Build idempotent backfill job for existing company approval tasks from seeded finance data

acceptanceCriteria: A database migration creates approval workflow tables and fields with indexes on companyId, assigneeId, status, and dueDate.
The system supports approval targets of bill, payment, and exception with workflow states pending, approved, rejected, and escalated.
Threshold-based approval rules create pending approval tasks automatically when a bill or payment exceeds configured limits.
GET /api/finance/approvals/pending returns only tenant-scoped pending and escalated approval tasks with target type, target id, assignee, due date, and status.
Existing bill records remain valid after migration even when no approval task exists for the bill.
A backfill job creates approval tasks for existing mock bills that match approval rule criteria without creating duplicates on repeated runs.

stories.title: Add budget and forecast planning models with seeded baseline data

tasks.title: Create Budget and Forecast entities with EF Core mappings, constraints, and migrations
Implement budget CRUD APIs and forecast retrieval APIs with tenant and period filters
Build baseline planning seed service for current and newly onboarded companies

acceptanceCriteria: A database migration creates budget and forecast tables with uniqueness constraints covering companyId, period, accountId, version, and optional costCenterId.
Budget APIs support create, update, list, and version-filtered retrieval of month-level budget records by company and period.
Forecast APIs return month-level forecast values by account and version for a requested company and period range.
Seed logic creates baseline budget and forecast data for newly created companies and backfills missing planning data for already onboarded companies.
Budget and forecast records reference valid chart-of-accounts accounts already present for the tenant.
Repeated execution of seed or backfill logic does not create duplicate budget or forecast rows for the same company, period, account, version, and cost center.

stories.title: Deliver actual vs budget and actual vs forecast variance services

tasks.title: Implement variance aggregation service for actual vs budget and actual vs forecast comparisons
Expose variance API endpoints with grouping by period, account, category, and optional cost center
Add integration tests for seeded monthly variance calculations and tenant isolation

acceptanceCriteria: Variance services aggregate actuals from ledger data for a requested company and period and compare them against budget and forecast values.
The API returns variance amount and variance percentage grouped by period and account, and includes cost center grouping when cost centers are enabled for the tenant.
For a fixed seeded dataset, variance calculations match expected values in automated integration tests for at least one monthly period.
If summary tables are introduced, a documented refresh job populates them from ledger or snapshot data and can be rerun safely.
The variance API excludes data from other tenants and returns empty results rather than errors when no budget or forecast exists for a requested slice.

stories.title: Add finance insight services and migration-safe rollout verification

tasks.title: Implement finance insight aggregation services and narrative-ready DTOs
Add insight API endpoints and optional snapshot refresh job with retention handling
Create migration verification tests and startup pending-migration guard for dev and test
Build admin bootstrap endpoint for idempotent finance planning and approval backfill reruns

acceptanceCriteria: Insight services compute top expenses, revenue trend, burn rate, overdue customer risk, and payable pressure for a tenant using current finance data.
An insight API returns structured metrics and narrative-ready fields for agent consumption with a generatedAt timestamp.
If insight cache or materialization tables are added, migrations create them and a background refresh job updates snapshots without duplicate rows for the same tenant and snapshot key.
Automated migration tests cover clean database migration, migration from the current mock finance schema, and migration of a partially seeded company.
Startup validation in dev and test environments fails fast when pending migrations exist.
A safe admin-triggered bootstrap operation can rerun planning and approval backfills for an existing company without duplicating seeded records.

epics.title: EPIC-28 — Lean Financial Simulation Consistency Layer and Deterministic Operational Finance Scenarios

stories.title: Implement simulation event causality and object linkage across invoices, bills, payments, assets, and cash

tasks.title: Add SimulationEventRecord persistence model with deterministic event identity and source metadata
Extend finance entities with source event references and causal linkage fields
Persist cash delta records with before and after values during event execution

acceptanceCriteria: Every simulated invoice, bill, payment, asset, and cash-affecting transaction stores a source simulation event reference.
For any payment allocation, the system can resolve the linked payment, target document, and originating simulation event in a single query path.
Cash-affecting events persist before/after cash values and the delta amount for the affected company and simulation date.
Replaying the same company seed and start date produces the same event identifiers, object references, and causal links.

stories.title: Build deterministic operational finance scenario generation for receivables, payables, recurring costs, and asset purchases

tasks.title: Implement seeded scenario generator for customer invoice issuance and collection behavior
Implement seeded recurring cost and supplier bill generation rules by company profile
Implement seeded asset purchase event generation with cash and payable funding modes

acceptanceCriteria: A seeded simulation run generates invoices, bills, recurring costs, and asset purchases with deterministic dates, amounts, counterparties, and statuses.
Generated invoices create receivable exposure until settled by incoming payments.
Generated bills create payable exposure until settled by outgoing payments.
Asset purchase events always create an asset record and either reduce cash immediately or create a payable based on configured funding behavior.

stories.title: Expose derived operational finance state for dashboards, agents, and debug workflows

tasks.title: Implement finance summary projection service from invoices, bills, payments, assets, and cash snapshots
Add application queries or API endpoints for dashboard, agent context, and simulation debug finance summaries
Add consistency checks validating projection totals against source simulation records

acceptanceCriteria: The application exposes queryable metrics for current cash, accounts receivable, overdue receivables, accounts payable, overdue payables, monthly revenue, monthly costs, and recent asset purchases.
Derived metrics match the underlying simulated document, payment, and cash state for the same company and point in time.
Point-in-time finance summary queries return consistent results for repeated reads against the same simulation state.
Finance summary queries complete within agreed service thresholds for seeded companies in local and test environments.

stories.title: Add deterministic regression coverage for core financial cause-effect flows and replayability

tasks.title: Add integration tests for invoice, bill, payment, cash, and overdue progression flows
Add deterministic replay tests for seeded scenario generation and event execution
Add projection regression tests for finance summary outputs used by UI and agents

acceptanceCriteria: Automated tests verify invoice issuance increases receivables, invoice payment increases cash and reduces receivables, bill receipt increases payables, and bill payment decreases cash and reduces payables.
Automated tests verify overdue invoice and bill states emerge from due dates and unpaid balances after time progression.
Automated tests verify recurring costs and asset purchases produce expected payable or cash effects.
Automated tests verify the same seed, profile, and start date produce identical event timelines and derived finance summaries.
